executivePositions.title: Chief Operating Officer
executivePositions.location: Remote with regular in-person leadership meetings

benefits.academyCertifications: All certifications provided at no cost

requirements.experience: 5+ years in operations management or similar leadership role

executivePositions.description: The Chief Operating Officer oversees day-to-day operations across all five subsidiary entities. This role coordinates between department leads, ensures efficient resource allocation, and drives operational excellence throughout the organization.
responsibilities: Direct supervision of Operations Manager, HR Lead, QA/QC Lead, Purchasing Lead, and Platform Administrator
Develop and implement operational policies and procedures
Monitor organizational performance metrics and KPIs
Coordinate cross-entity projects and initiatives
Manage capacity planning and resource allocation
Report operational status to CEO/Matriarch
Drive continuous process improvement
Ensure alignment between entity operations and organizational strategy

executivePositions.title: Executive Director
executivePositions.entity: LuvOnPurpose Outreach Temple and Academy Society, Inc.

benefits.academyCertifications: All certifications provided at no cost

requirements.experience: 3+ years in nonprofit leadership or program management

executivePositions.description: The Executive Director provides overall leadership for the 508(c)(1)(a) faith-based organization, ensuring program excellence, grant compliance, and community impact. This role is essential for nonprofit credibility with funders and partners.
responsibilities: Overall leadership of Temple/Academy operations
Grant compliance and reporting for nonprofit programs
Community and donor relationship management
Program development and oversight
Board liaison and governance support
Direct supervision of Education Director and Outreach Coordinator
Represent organization at community events and with partners
Develop and maintain relationships with funders
executivePositions.notes: Required for nonprofit credibility with funders

filledExecutivePositions.title: Chief Executive Officer / Matriarch
filledExecutivePositions.entity: Calea Freeman Family Trust / The L.A.W.S. Collective, LLC
filledExecutivePositions.description: Vision and strategic direction for all entities. Final authority on major decisions. Trust governance and succession planning.

filledExecutivePositions.title: Chief Financial Officer
filledExecutivePositions.description: Financial strategy and planning across all entities. Budget development and monitoring. Grant financial management and compliance. Token economy oversight.
responsibilities: Financial strategy and planning across all entities
Budget development and monitoring
Grant financial management and compliance
Token economy oversight
Financial reporting and analysis
Banking and investment relationships

filledExecutivePositions.title: Chief Education Officer
crossEntityScope: LuvOnPurpose Outreach Temple and Academy Society, Inc.
filledExecutivePositions.description: The Chief Education Officer provides strategic leadership for all education, training, and professional development initiatives across all subsidiary entities. This executive role oversees curriculum development, instructor management, certification programs, and educational partnerships while leveraging expertise in both education and criminal justice.
responsibilities: Strategic oversight of all education and training programs across entities
Academy curriculum development and quality assurance
Instructor recruitment, training, and management
Certification program design and administration
Educational partnership development
L.A.W.S. AIR Pillar (Education) leadership
Training program development for LAWS, LLC platform
Education liaison coordination with Real-Eye-Nation media
Legal/Justice advisory support (Criminal Justice expertise)
Student progress tracking and outcome measurement
Grant compliance for education-related funding

qualifications.experience: 5+ years in education leadership or training management

additionalStipends.description: Advisory support leveraging Criminal Justice expertise

stipendPolicy.description: Stipends are additional compensation provided to employees who take on duties beyond their primary role. Stipends are separate from base salary and may be adjusted based on workload and organizational needs.
stipendPolicy.eligibility: Any employee may receive stipends for additional cross-entity or specialized duties approved by leadership.

ranges.examples: Pillar leadership, liaison roles, advisory functions
Department oversight, specialized expertise, executive advisory

stipendPolicy.paymentSchedule: Stipends are paid monthly as part of regular payroll or quarterly based on agreement.
stipendPolicy.reviewFrequency: Stipend assignments are reviewed annually or when role responsibilities change.

positions.title: HR Manager

benefits.academyCertifications: All certifications provided at no cost

requirements.experience: 3+ years in HR, recruiting, or people management

positions.description: The HR Manager builds and maintains the human capital infrastructure across all five subsidiary entities. This role oversees talent acquisition, onboarding, training coordination, performance management, and employee relations.
responsibilities: Develop and execute recruitment strategies
Design and manage comprehensive onboarding programs
Coordinate training paths with Academy leadership
Implement performance evaluation frameworks
Serve as first point of contact for workplace concerns
Maintain HR records and ensure compliance
Support benefits administration

positions.title: QA/QC Manager

benefits.academyCertifications: All certifications provided at no cost

requirements.experience: 3+ years in quality assurance, compliance, or process management

positions.description: The QA/QC Manager establishes and maintains quality standards across all subsidiary entities. This role ensures deliverables meet organizational standards and grant compliance requirements are satisfied.
responsibilities: Define quality criteria and acceptance standards
Review work outputs before delivery
Develop and maintain Standard Operating Procedures
Ensure adherence to grant requirements
Lead continuous improvement initiatives
Conduct regular audits and assessments
Train staff on quality procedures

positions.title: Purchasing Manager
positions.location: Remote with vendor site visits as needed

requirements.experience: 2+ years in purchasing, procurement, or supply chain

positions.description: The Purchasing Manager manages procurement operations across all subsidiary entities. This role handles vendor relationships, cost control, inventory tracking, and grant compliance for purchasing activities.
responsibilities: Identify, evaluate, and select vendors
Negotiate contracts and pricing
Provide cost estimates for grant proposals
Maintain inventory tracking system
Ensure purchases comply with grant requirements
Process purchase orders
Report purchasing metrics and cost savings

positions.title: Operations Manager

requirements.experience: 3+ years in operations, project management, or coordination

positions.description: The Operations Manager ensures smooth day-to-day operations across all subsidiary entities. This role coordinates workflows, monitors business systems, identifies bottlenecks, and provides support to team members.
responsibilities: Coordinate workflows between entities
Monitor business systems for performance
Identify and resolve operational bottlenecks
Provide administrative support to team members
Track operational KPIs and report to leadership
Manage cross-functional projects
Maintain operational documentation

positions.title: Outreach Coordinator
positions.entity: LuvOnPurpose Outreach Temple and Academy Society, Inc.

requirements.experience: 2+ years in community outreach, event planning, or nonprofit work

positions.description: The Outreach Coordinator connects the organization with the broader community through events, partnerships, and relationship building. This role plans community events, builds partner relationships, and coordinates volunteers.
responsibilities: Plan and execute community events
Build relationships with community organizations
Recruit and manage volunteers
Track program participation and outcomes
Coordinate with Media team on event promotion
Represent organization at community gatherings
Support grant-funded community initiatives

positions.title: Content Creator / Media Assistant

requirements.experience: Portfolio required

positions.description: The Content Creator supports the media production mission by creating social media content, documenting events, editing videos, and writing copy for marketing materials.
responsibilities: Create engaging social media content
Document events and programs through photo/video
Edit videos for tutorials and promotional content
Write copy for posts, emails, and marketing materials
Support digital asset creation
Manage content calendar
Support live streaming and virtual events

positions.title: Academy Instructor / Curriculum Developer
positions.entity: LuvOnPurpose Outreach Temple and Academy Society, Inc.

benefits.academyCertifications: All certifications provided at no cost

requirements.experience: Expertise in at least one subject area

positions.description: The Academy Instructor delivers educational content and develops new courses for the LuvOnPurpose Academy. This role teaches courses, creates curriculum, develops assessments, and tracks student progress.
responsibilities: Deliver courses in assigned subject areas
Design new courses based on organizational needs
Create assessments and track student progress
Issue certificates upon completion
Continuously improve based on feedback
Provide student support and mentorship
Participate in curriculum planning

positions.title: Grant Writer / Proposal Specialist

requirements.experience: 2+ years in grant writing or proposal development

positions.description: The Grant Writer identifies funding opportunities and writes compelling proposals to secure grants for all subsidiary entities. This role researches opportunities, develops narratives, creates budgets, and manages deadlines.
responsibilities: Research grant opportunities matched to each entity
Write compelling narratives and proposals
Develop budgets with Purchasing and Finance
Manage multiple concurrent applications
Track outcomes and prepare progress reports
Manage grant calendar and deadlines
Coordinate with program staff for requirements

positions.title: Platform Administrator

requirements.experience: 2+ years in IT administration or technical support

positions.description: The Platform Administrator maintains and improves the technology infrastructure of the organization. This role monitors system health, manages user accounts, implements new features, and ensures security.
responsibilities: Monitor platform health and performance
Create and manage user accounts
Implement new features as platform grows
Ensure security of sensitive data
Train users on platform features
Document technical procedures
Provide technical support to team

positions.title: Community Programs Coordinator

requirements.experience: 2+ years in program management or community development

positions.description: The Community Programs Coordinator develops and manages programs based on the L.A.W.S. framework (Land, Air, Water, Self) that help community members reconnect with their roots, gain knowledge, find balance, and discover purpose.
responsibilities: Design programs based on L.A.W.S. principles
Recruit and manage program participants
Coordinate with partner organizations
Track outcomes for grant reporting
Build community among program alumni
Manage program budgets
Build relationships with community partners

positions.title: Health Manager

requirements.experience: 5+ years in health administration, wellness program management, or healthcare coordination

positions.description: The Health Manager oversees all health and wellness initiatives aligned with the WATER pillar of the L.A.W.S. framework. This role develops and manages wellness programs, coordinates healthcare resources, and ensures community members have access to healing and balance services.
responsibilities: Develop and implement wellness programs aligned with WATER pillar principles
Coordinate healthcare resources and referral networks
Manage health-related grants and funding
Supervise Health Operations Coordinator
Track health outcomes and program effectiveness
Build partnerships with healthcare providers and wellness organizations
Ensure compliance with health-related regulations
Design mental health and emotional wellness support programs
Coordinate with Education department on health literacy curriculum
Report health metrics and outcomes to leadership

positions.title: Procurement Manager

requirements.experience: 7+ years in procurement, supply chain management, or strategic sourcing

positions.description: The Procurement Manager leads strategic procurement operations across all subsidiary entities. This senior role develops procurement strategies, manages major vendor relationships, ensures grant compliance, and optimizes organizational spending.
responsibilities: Develop and implement procurement strategies across all entities
Lead strategic sourcing initiatives and major contract negotiations
Manage vendor relationships and performance evaluations
Ensure procurement compliance with grant requirements and regulations
Supervise Purchasing Manager and procurement staff
Analyze spending patterns and identify cost-saving opportunities
Develop procurement policies and procedures
Coordinate with Finance on budget planning and forecasting
Manage RFP/RFQ processes for major purchases
Report procurement metrics and savings to leadership

positions.title: Contracts Manager

requirements.experience: 7+ years in contract administration, contract management, or legal operations

positions.description: The Contracts Manager leads contract administration and management across all subsidiary entities. This senior role handles contract drafting, review, negotiation, compliance monitoring, and risk management for all organizational agreements.
responsibilities: Draft, review, and negotiate contracts across all entities
Manage vendor and supplier contract lifecycle
Ensure grant contract compliance and reporting
Administer employment agreements and HR contracts
Handle lease, property, and real estate contracts
Develop contract templates and standard terms
Monitor contract performance and renewals
Assess and mitigate contractual risks
Coordinate with legal counsel on complex agreements
Supervise Contracts Operations Coordinator
Maintain contract database and documentation
Report contract metrics and compliance status to leadership

positions.title: Design Manager

requirements.experience: 5+ years in graphic design, brand management, or creative direction

positions.description: The Design Manager leads all visual design and branding initiatives across the organization. This role establishes brand identity, creates design systems, and ensures visual consistency across all entities, marketing materials, and digital platforms.
responsibilities: Develop and maintain brand identity and design systems for all entities
Create visual designs for marketing, web, print, and digital platforms
Lead design projects from concept to completion
Supervise Design Operations Coordinator and creative staff
Establish design standards and style guides
Collaborate with Marketing and Media teams on campaigns
Manage design project timelines and deliverables
Review and approve all design work for brand consistency
Stay current with design trends and tools
Support Academy with course materials and visual content

positions.title: Media Manager

requirements.experience: 5+ years in media production, video production, or content management

positions.description: The Media Manager leads all media production and content creation initiatives for Real-Eye-Nation and the broader organization. This role oversees video production, podcasts, social media content, and ensures consistent messaging across all media channels.
responsibilities: Develop and execute media content strategy across all platforms
Lead video production projects from pre-production to post
Manage podcast production and distribution
Supervise Media Operations Coordinator and content creators
Oversee social media content calendar and engagement
Coordinate with Design team on visual content
Manage media equipment and production resources
Track media metrics and optimize content performance
Build relationships with media partners and influencers
Support Academy with video courses and educational content

leadPositions.title: Lead Operations Coordinator

requirements.experience: 3+ years in operations or administrative leadership

leadPositions.description: The Lead Operations Coordinator oversees all department Operations Coordinator staff across the organization, ensuring efficient day-to-day operations. This role serves as the primary support to the executive team while supervising and coordinating the HR, QA/QC, Purchasing, Operations, and Education Operations Coordinator staff.
responsibilities: Supervise and lead all department Operations Coordinator staff (HR, QA/QC, Purchasing, Operations, Education)
Provide direct administrative and operational support to CEO, COO, and executive team
Coordinate daily operations and communications across all entities
Manage team schedules, assignments, and workload distribution
Develop and improve operational processes and procedures
Conduct team meetings and performance check-ins with Operations Coordinator staff
Serve as escalation point for operational issues across departments
Coordinate cross-department projects and communications
Prepare executive reports and operational summaries
Support executive meetings, board activities, and strategic initiatives
Train and onboard new Operations Coordinator staff
Ensure consistent quality and efficiency across all support functions

standard.name: Standard Benefits Package
includes: All Academy certifications at no cost
Token earnings based on role metrics

grantCompliant.name: Grant-Compliant Benefits Package
grantCompliant.description: For positions funded by grants requiring traditional benefits
grantCompliant.notes: Specific benefits vary by grant requirements

remoteWorkSupport.name: Remote Work Support Package
includes: Monthly utilities stipend ($75-150/month for internet and electricity)
Variable travel reimbursement (mileage, lodging, per diem for required travel)

outreachProfessional.name: Outreach & Professional Appearance Package
outreachProfessional.description: For public-facing and outreach positions that serve as organizational representatives
outreachProfessional.justification: Staff serve dual roles as outreach representatives and organizational ambassadors, requiring professional appearance for community events, partner meetings, and media appearances.

softwareSubscriptions.name: Software Subscriptions Package
softwareSubscriptions.description: Essential software tools for remote work productivity

creative.note: Adobe Creative Cloud for media roles

systemMaintenance.name: System Maintenance & IT Infrastructure
systemMaintenance.note: Budget as single organizational line item, not per-employee. Can also be included in indirect costs if organization has negotiated indirect rate.

grantBudgetSummary.description: Estimated annual costs per employee for grant budgeting purposes

utilitiesStipend.description: Monthly internet and electricity support for remote work

equipmentProvision.description: Laptop, phone, software licenses - one-time with annual maintenance

travelReimbursement.description: Variable based on role - mileage at federal rate, lodging, per diem

softwareSubscriptions.description: Productivity suite, communication, project management, role-specific tools, security

totalPerEmployeeRange.note: First year includes one-time equipment and setup costs. Does NOT include organizational IT infrastructure.

systemMaintenance.description: Cloud hosting, backup, IT support contract, domain/email, security monitoring - budgeted at organizational level, not per-employee
